steps.title: Find the width using the given base area
steps.content:
The base area is the product of length and width. Given base area = $28p$ and length = 12:
$$12w = 28p$$
$$w = \frac{28p}{12} = \frac{7p}{3}$$
The prism has dimensions: length = 12 cm, width = $\frac{7p}{3}$ cm, height = 4 cm.

steps.title: Apply the surface area formula for a rectangular prism
steps.content:
For a rectangular prism, $SA = 2(lw + lh + wh)$. Calculate each product:
$$lw = 12 \cdot \frac{7p}{3} = 28p$$
$$lh = 12 \cdot 4 = 48$$
$$wh = \frac{7p}{3} \cdot 4 = \frac{28p}{3}$$

steps.title: Simplify to find the surface area
steps.content:
Substitute into the formula:
$$SA = 2\left(28p + 48 + \frac{28p}{3}\right)$$
$$SA = 56p + 96 + \frac{56p}{3}$$
Combine the $p$ terms: $56p = \frac{168p}{3}$, so:
$$SA = \frac{168p}{3} + \frac{56p}{3} + 96 = \frac{224p}{3} + 96$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
